acceptance_criteria.text: Browser panel chrome reflects navigation state, shows the current URL in the panel title, uses an inline icon-only navigate control when the draft differs, and supports Enter navigation while preserving existing browser selectors.

proof.summary: Browser title derives from the loaded URL with a neutral fallback, and the browser panel preserves the required selectors while rendering the inline icon-only navigate control.
Browser smoke coverage passed in the full Playwright suite.

acceptance_criteria.text: Editor panels use Monaco instead of CodeMirror without regressing current file-open, edit, and save behavior, including Ctrl/Cmd+S and current language coverage.

proof.summary: EditorPanel mounts Monaco, wires model changes to app state, binds Ctrl/Cmd+S, and maps the handled file extensions to Monaco languages.
The Monaco runtime dependencies were added for the editor migration.
Playwright passed the file-open/editor/browser/git flow coverage after the Monaco fix.

acceptance_criteria.text: Monaco theme follows the shared app theme tokens and updates with the global theme instead of introducing an editor-only theme toggle.

proof.summary: Monaco theme colors are derived from root CSS variables and reapplied when root theme attributes change.
The shared root theme tokens and Monaco-aligned shell styles come from the existing app theme stylesheet.

acceptance_criteria.text: The projects sidebar can be collapsed to a materially narrower compact state and expanded again without losing active workspace awareness.

proof.summary: App adds an explicit collapse/expand affordance, narrows the sidebar in compact mode, and preserves active project/session context.

acceptance_criteria.text: The active session header becomes denser on large screens, keeping the session name and chips on one line where space allows while preserving key controls and test ids.

proof.summary: The header uses a slimmer large-screen layout while preserving the active-session, start, stop, and new-panel controls.

acceptance_criteria.text: Ctrl+Alt+Up and Ctrl+Alt+Down cycle sessions across projects with wraparound, updating the active project and active session together.

proof.summary: Workspace session order is flattened across projects and the global keydown handler binds Ctrl+Alt+Up/Down to wraparound session cycling.

acceptance_criteria.text: Ctrl+Alt+Left and Ctrl+Alt+Right cycle focused panels with wraparound, keeping focus styling and scrolling the target panel into view.

proof.summary: Panel cycling uses active-session panel order with wraparound, preserves focused styling, and scrolls the target panel into view.
Playwright passed the workspace panel polish coverage for the panel rail behavior.

acceptance_criteria.text: Ctrl+Alt+N opens the Add Panel flow in browser-safe fashion, and shortcut hints appear in hover tooltips where available, including the new panel button and icon-only browser navigate control.

proof.summary: The global keydown handler binds Ctrl+Alt+N to open the Add Panel dialog and exposes shortcut hints on the relevant controls.
The browser-mode Playwright suite passed with the updated panel controls in place.

known_gaps: No dedicated lint script exists in package.json.
No dedicated unit-test script exists in package.json.
The production build emits a non-blocking Vite chunk-size warning recorded in raw/build.txt.
